model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Iowa State is the slight pick because this projects as a very tight Sweet 16 game between two elite defensive teams, but the Cyclones appear to have the stronger overall ceiling and a bit more offensive punch than Virginia. Virginia has a very credible path if it controls tempo and turns this into a half-court grind, which keeps confidence modest. Even with Keshon Gilbert still out, Iowa State’s defensive pressure and more balanced profile make it the narrower advance choice.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced edge to Iowa State in a low-margin defensive matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Virginia’s available ratings are excellent and suggest a true contender, but matchup reporting and broader 2026 context point to Iowa State as a similarly elite team with slightly more offensive upside. The biggest confirmed availability item is Gilbert remaining out for Iowa State, which narrows the gap and keeps this close, yet not enough to flip the pick to Virginia.
evidence: Virginia ratings found: KenPom No. 13, Torvik No. 14, with adj. offense around No. 28-31 and adj. defense around No. 14.
Web results identified confirmed Iowa State injury news that Keshon Gilbert is out for the NCAA tournament with a groin injury.
Matchup coverage described Iowa State-Virginia as a premier offense-vs-defense style game, indicating Iowa State brings more scoring upside while Virginia relies more on control and execution.
No comparably significant Virginia availability issue was confirmed in the tool results, so the decision came down to team quality, style, and the underdog path rather than a late injury swing.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
